import AnimatedPlaceholderText from "@/components/AnimatedPlaceholderText"; import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"; import React from "react"; import { motion, useMotionTemplate, useMotionValue } from "motion/react"; interface InputProps extends React.InputHTMLAttributes { useAnimatedPlaceholder?: boolean; } export const Input = React.forwardRef( ({ className, type, useAnimatedPlaceholder, ...props }, ref) => { const [inputValue, setInputValue] = React.useState(""); const radius = 100; const [visible, setVisible] = React.useState(false); const mouseX = useMotionValue(0); const mouseY = useMotionValue(0); function handleMouseMove({ currentTarget, clientX, clientY }: any) { const { left, top } = currentTarget.getBoundingClientRect(); mouseX.set(clientX - left); mouseY.set(clientY - top); } return ( setVisible(true)} onMouseLeave={() => setVisible(false)} className="group/input relative rounded-xl p-[2px] transition duration-300" > {/* Show animated placeholder only if input is empty */} {useAnimatedPlaceholder && !inputValue && ( )} { setInputValue(e.target.value); props.onChange?.(e); }} className={cn( `shadow-input placeholder:text-zinc-400 flex h-10 w-full rounded-xl border-none px-3 py-2 text-lg transition duration-400 group-hover/input:shadow-none file:border-0 file:bg-transparent file:text-lg file:font-medium focus-visible:ring-[2px] focus-visible:outline-none disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50 bg-zinc-900 text-gray-100 dark:shadow-[0px_0px_2px_1px_rgba(189,147,249,0.25)] focus-visible:ring-[#FF79C6]`, className, )} ref={ref} placeholder="" // keep empty {...props} /> ); } ); frontend/src/components/ui/input.tsx

content.thinking:
The user wants me to change the hover color to pink in the input component. Let me analyze the file content:

Looking at the file, I can see there are two places where hover/shadow effects are defined:

1. In the motion.div style:
```javascript
background: useMotionTemplate`
    radial-gradient(
        ${visible ? radius + "px" : "0px"} circle at ${mouseX}px ${mouseY}px,
        #bd93f9,
        transparent 80%
    )
`
```
This uses `#bd93f9` which is a purple color.

2. In the input's className:
```javascript
className={cn(
    `shadow-input placeholder:text-zinc-400
    flex h-10 w-full rounded-xl border-none
    px-3 py-2 text-lg transition duration-400
    group-hover/input:shadow-none
    file:border-0 file:bg-transparent file:text-lg file:font-medium
    focus-visible:ring-[2px] focus-visible:outline-none
    disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50
    bg-zinc-900 text-gray-100
    dark:shadow-[0px_0px_2px_1px_rgba(189,147,249,0.25)]
    focus-visible:ring-[#FF79C6]`,
```

The user mentions "change the colour of hover to pink". 

Looking at the code:
- The focus-visible:ring-[#FF79C6] is already using a pink color (#FF79C6 is a pink shade)
- The dark:shadow uses rgba(189,147,249,0.25) which is purple (189,147,249 is the purple color from the Dracula theme)
- The radial gradient uses #bd93f9 which is also a purple color (Dracula purple)

If the user wants to change the hover color to pink, I think they want to change the radial gradient effect (which shows on hover) from purple to pink.


I'll replace the purple colors with a vibrant pink shade. The hex code #FF69B4 (hot pink) or #FF1493 (deep pink) would work well to create a pink hover effect. I'll modify the radial gradient and shadow properties to incorporate this new pink color, ensuring the hover state looks intentional and visually appealing.
